channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: CD Projekt Red's Hadar has moved onto the prototyping stage age, as The Witcher 4 team expands
item.content:encoded:
"Work on the project is progressing dynamically"

Project Hadar… It's a completely new game from CD Projekt Red, one that the Cyberpunk 2077 studio has been tinkering away at in the background for a while now. And yet! We know next to nothing about it, not even the genre (yes I know it's probably an RPG given CD Projekt's whole thing, I'm creating mystique here). But today was CD Projekt's most recent earnings report, so now we know… not much much, though we do have a better idea of where it's at.

CD Projekt joint CEO Michał Nowakowski shared during a presentation today that "work on the project is progressing dynamically, and the team has established the foundation of this entirely new IP. Now, we have clearly-defined distinguishing features of this world, and established key pillars that will not only provide the foundation for the game itself, but also potentially for other future products. Of course this does not mean the end of work on IP itself, which will naturally continue to evolve, and develop on many fronts over time."

"The team has established the foundations of this entirely new IP. Now, we are designing specific elements that might be included in the game, creating multiple prototypes and implementing them directly in Unreal Engine." - said @michalnowakow during FY 2025 earnings conference pic.twitter.com/jMiwsIvwW5

— CD PROJEKT IR (@CDPROJEKTRED_IR) March 19, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

While still keeping pretty tight lipped about it, Nowakowski continued to explain that the team working on Hadar is working on various prototypes, testing "specific elements" that may (or may not) make it into the final game. Essentially, it sounds like they're very early into figuring out what the game actually is, both narratively and mechanically.

Not much to go off of overall, but a brief update was offered towards The Witcher 4's progress, that being that the dev team is bigger now, up to 499 staff members, an increase of almost 50 people since October last year. There's more folks on Cyberpunk 2 now too, but only a handful comparatively. With all this talk about sequels, you can't help but wonder about that speculated upcoming Witcher 3 DLC. Shame CD Projekt didn't mention anything about that.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/cd-projekt-reds-hadar-has-moved-onto-the-prototyping-stage-age-as-the-witcher-4-team-expands
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon's Cryo Archive drops tomorrow and it sounds like almost every aspect of it will be a pain in the shell
item.content:encoded:
Well, you all did it, Cryo Archive has been unlocked in Marathon. Bungie detailed the new mode in a blog post earlier today, where it shared an important thing to keep in mind with the new map: it's only available to attempt on weekends. Aye, you'll need to forgo any social plans you have out in the real world to tackle this one.

At the very least, "weekend" sounds like it'll include Friday, as the new map actually goes live tomorrow, March 20th. But there's another important thing to keep in mind, and that's the fact it also sounds like Cryo Archive can't be taken on solo. "It's designed to be taken on by a team of Runners, so you'll need a crew of three," the blog post explains. This can of course be with a couple of mates, but if you typically fly solo, you'll have to fill your team up with randos for this one.

Anyway, what's this map actually like? "In game, Cryo Archive takes you through six interconnected wings with a central hub. Traverse from wing-to-hub and back again in search of frozen Vaults containing powerful loot and dark secrets. Battle your way through intense UESC security, decked out Runner crews, and a ship that seems hellbent on keeping you out."

It is specifically designed to be challenging, more so than normal, and will apparently take a lot of runs to fully learn. Bungie also likens Cryo Archive to raids, saying it has similarly designed "puzzle solving and layered mechanics wrapped around brutal extraction stakes." Part of this will involve gaining certain levels of security clearance, which in a run will let you access more and more rooms.

There's also seven vaults to try and get into, with specific challenges or requirements in place you'll have to see to first. Once you're in, you'll find things like high-end containers, gold weapons, and "a path toward assembling a DN~~~%^&*()8167f48f-e253-4e2f-be4c-2077b646d531+{}:"<>?~~ that leads deeper into the Marathon's secrets and towa~~~~%^&*()_+{}:"<>?~~~~~~~."

Getting out of Cryo Archive will apparently be tougher than normal too, but figuring out how to get off will be up to you. And for quest-enjoyers, you'll have access to seven unique contracts to try out. Sounds pretty full on! Better you than me, I'll stick to Peggle.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/marathons-cryo-archive-drops-tomorrow-and-it-sounds-like-almost-every-aspect-of-it-will-be-a-pain-in-the-shell
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
